# 4. Clinical Applications {background-color="#6880af"}

<style>
/* The disease slide is a flex column: heading + subline take their natural height,
   the .cards grid takes ALL the rest. Dense slides (.fill) split that leftover space
   into equal rows (grid-auto-rows:1fr) so they fill top-to-bottom with no whitespace
   and no overflow; sparse slides (.center) use a capped row height and sit centred.
   --cols fills the width; --cardw caps card width on sparse slides; object-fit:contain
   resizes each figure to fit without cropping; overflow:hidden is a hard stop. */
.reveal section.cards-slide { overflow:hidden; }
.cards { width:100%; margin:0.3rem auto 0; overflow:hidden; gap:0.5rem; }
/* dense: grid with a FIXED row height in logical px of the 1244x750 slide, so the
   layout is identical at every window size (Reveal scales the whole slide uniformly) */
.cards.fill { display:grid; justify-content:center; grid-auto-rows:var(--rowh,175px);
              grid-template-columns:repeat(var(--cols,4), minmax(0, 1fr)); }
/* sparse: a few capped-width cards of fixed height, centred horizontally */
.cards.center { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; justify-content:center; align-content:flex-start; }
.cards.center .card { flex:1 1 var(--cardw,340px); max-width:var(--cardmax,560px); height:var(--rowh,340px); }
.cards .card { display:flex; flex-direction:column; min-height:0; overflow:hidden;
         border:1px solid #d4dde4; border-radius:9px; background:#fff; box-shadow:0 1px 5px rgba(40,60,90,.14); }
.cards .card > p:has(img), .cards .card .cardimg { margin:0; flex:1 1 auto; min-height:0; display:flex; }
.cards .card img { width:100%; height:100%; object-fit:contain;
         background:#f4f7f9; border-bottom:1px solid #e6edf2; padding:0.15rem; }
.cards .card .noimg { flex:1 1 auto; min-height:0; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center;
         text-align:center; padding:0.3rem; background:#dbe8df; color:#36684b; font-style:italic; font-size:0.62rem; }
.cards .card .cardbody { flex:0 0 auto; padding:0.3rem 0.5rem 0.4rem; font-size:0.5rem; line-height:1.2; }
.cards .card .cardbody p { margin:0.08rem 0 0; }
.cards .card .cardbody p:last-child { display:-webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp:3;
         -webkit-box-orient:vertical; overflow:hidden; }   /* clamp blurb so cards stay uniform */
.cards .card .cardbody strong { color:#23364a; }
.cards .card .cardbody .mdl { color:#5a7d80; font-style:italic; }
</style>

## From mechanism to the clinic

Brain disorders are disorders of brain **dynamics**. A *personalized virtual brain* turns a patient's scans into a working model — to expose a mechanism we cannot measure, and to rehearse a treatment before it reaches the patient.

```{dot}
//| fig-align: center
//| fig-height: 3.0
digraph recipe {
    rankdir=LR; bgcolor="transparent"; nodesep=0.30; ranksep=0.80;
    node [shape=box, style="filled,rounded", fontname="Helvetica", fontsize=13,
          penwidth=1.4, margin="0.20,0.15", color="#5ea8d4", fontcolor="#1c2b3a"];
    edge [color="#6880af", penwidth=1.7, arrowsize=0.85, fontname="Helvetica",
          fontsize=11, fontcolor="#6880af"];

    data   [label="Patient data\nMRI · dMRI · EEG / MEG", fillcolor="#e2f2fb"];
    model  [label="Personalized\nvirtual brain", fillcolor="#bee0f4"];
    mech   [label="Hidden mechanism\nexcitability · E/I · connectivity", fillcolor="#cfe8d6"];
    clinic [label="Clinical use\ndiagnose · predict · plan therapy", fillcolor="#f0e0c4"];

    data -> model  [label=" build "];
    model -> mech  [label=" simulate & fit "];
    mech -> clinic [label=" infer "];
    clinic -> data [label=" personalize ", constraint=false, style=dashed,
                    color="#9ab5b8", fontcolor="#9ab5b8"];
}
```

::: {.notes}
**One idea for the whole chapter:** brain disorders are disorders of *dynamics* — so rebuild each patient's brain as a network model and let it run.

- **Build** — patient scans (MRI · dMRI · EEG/MEG) → a personalized virtual brain
- **Simulate & fit** — match the model to that patient's data
- **Infer** — read off a mechanism you *can't* measure directly: excitability · E/I balance · effective connectivity
- **Use** — predict an outcome, or rehearse a stimulation/therapy *in silico* before touching the patient

The dashed arrow = the personalized-medicine loop: the clinical question shapes the next measurement.
Next slides: where this recipe has actually reached patients, disease by disease.
:::

## The clinical landscape — ICD-11 areas → node dynamical models

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-align: center
# Figure generated live from the clinical ontology addon (tvb-o-clinical.ttl):
# ICD-11 chapter --skos:broader-- clinical domain (ICD-11 + MeSH)
#   --tvbc:usesNodeDynamics--> node dynamical model.  Nothing is hard-coded:
# every node/edge/count is read from the Turtle with SPARQL, so the picture
# always matches the ontology.
from pathlib import Path
import textwrap, graphviz, rdflib
from IPython.display import SVG, display

def _esc(s):
    return str(s).replace("&", "&amp;").replace("<", "&lt;").replace(">", "&gt;")

TTL = next(p for p in [Path("_dev/tvb-o-clinical.ttl"),
                       Path("../_dev/tvb-o-clinical.ttl")] if p.exists())
g = rdflib.Graph(); g.parse(TTL, format="turtle")
Q = ("PREFIX tvbc:<https://w3id.org/tvbo/clinical/> "
     "PREFIX skos:<http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#>")

chapters = {str(c): (str(n), str(l)) for c, n, l in g.query(Q +
    " SELECT ?c ?n ?l WHERE { ?c a skos:Concept ; skos:notation ?n ; skos:prefLabel ?l }")}
domains = {}
for d, lab, code, chap in g.query(Q + " SELECT ?d ?lab ?code ?chap WHERE { ?d a tvbc:ClinicalDomain ;"
        " skos:prefLabel ?lab ; tvbc:icd11Code ?code ; skos:broader ?chap }"):
    mesh = list(g.query(Q + " SELECT ?m WHERE { <%s> skos:exactMatch ?m }" % d))
    mesh = str(mesh[0][0]).rsplit("/", 1)[-1] if mesh else ""
    n = len(list(g.query(Q + " SELECT ?s WHERE { ?s tvbc:appliesToClinicalDomain <%s> }" % d)))
    domains[str(d)] = dict(label=str(lab), code=str(code), mesh=mesh, chap=str(chap), n=n)
models = {str(m): str(l) for m, l in g.query(Q +
    " SELECT ?m ?l WHERE { ?m a tvbc:NodeDynamicsModel ; skos:prefLabel ?l }")}
dm = {(str(d), str(m)): int(n) for d, m, n in g.query(Q + " SELECT ?d ?m (COUNT(?s) AS ?n) WHERE {"
        " ?s tvbc:appliesToClinicalDomain ?d ; tvbc:usesNodeDynamics ?m } GROUP BY ?d ?m")}

CFILL = {"08": "#bee0f4", "02": "#e8d4b0", "06": "#b0c0d8", "22": "#d8c0c0"}
CLINE = {"08": "#7ec0e0", "02": "#c8a870", "06": "#7898b8", "22": "#b08a8a"}
DFILL = {"08": "#e2f2fb", "02": "#f5ecd8", "06": "#dde6f0", "22": "#efe0e0"}
SHORT = {"08": "Nervous system", "02": "Neoplasms", "06": "Mental / behavioral", "22": "Injury"}
ORDER = ["08", "02", "06", "22"]; co = lambda nt: ORDER.index(nt) if nt in ORDER else 9
mtot = {}
for (d, m), n in dm.items():
    mtot[m] = mtot.get(m, 0) + n

dot = graphviz.Digraph("clinical")
dot.attr(rankdir="LR", bgcolor="transparent", nodesep="0.10", ranksep="0.95",
         fontname="Helvetica", splines="spline", pad="0.05", size="11.5,5.4")
dot.attr("node", fontname="Helvetica", penwidth="1.3", margin="0.10,0.045")
dot.attr("edge", color="#9ab5b8", penwidth="1.0", arrowsize="0.55",
         fontname="Helvetica", fontsize="9", fontcolor="#5a7d80")
with dot.subgraph() as s:                                   # column 1 — ICD-11 chapters
    s.attr(rank="same")
    for nt, _l in sorted(chapters.values(), key=lambda v: co(v[0])):
        s.node("c" + nt, f"<<b>{nt}</b><br/>{_esc(SHORT.get(nt, ''))}>", shape="box",
               style="filled,rounded", fillcolor=CFILL.get(nt, "#ccc"),
               color=CLINE.get(nt, "#999"), fontsize="12")
nid = {}
with dot.subgraph() as s:                                   # column 2 — clinical domains
    s.attr(rank="same")
    for i, (d, v) in enumerate(sorted(domains.items(),
            key=lambda kv: (co(chapters[kv[1]["chap"]][0]), -kv[1]["n"]))):
        nid[d] = f"d{i}"; nt = chapters[v["chap"]][0]
        meta = v["code"] + (f" · MeSH {v['mesh']}" if v["mesh"] else "")
        s.node(nid[d], f"<<b>{_esc(v['label'])}</b>"
               f"<br/><font point-size=\"8.5\">{_esc(meta)}</font>"
               f"<br/><font point-size=\"8.5\">{v['n']} studies</font>>", shape="box",
               style="filled,rounded", fillcolor=DFILL.get(nt, "#eee"),
               color=CLINE.get(nt, "#999"), fontsize="11")
mid = {}
with dot.subgraph() as s:                                   # column 3 — node dynamical models
    s.attr(rank="same")
    for j, (m, lab) in enumerate(sorted(models.items(), key=lambda kv: (-mtot.get(kv[0], 0), kv[1]))):
        mid[m] = f"m{j}"
        s.node(mid[m], "\\n".join(textwrap.wrap(lab, 22)), shape="note",
               style="filled", fillcolor="#a8d8b4", color="#70b480", fontsize="10.5")
for d, v in domains.items():                                # skos:broader
    dot.edge("c" + chapters[v["chap"]][0], nid[d])
for (d, m), n in dm.items():                                # tvbc:usesNodeDynamics
    dot.edge(nid[d], mid[m], label=(str(n) if n > 1 else ""), penwidth=f"{0.8 + 0.45 * n:.2f}")
dot.attr(label=f"\\nGenerated from tvb-o-clinical.ttl · "
         f"{sum(v['n'] for v in domains.values())} studies · {len(domains)} clinical domains · "
         f"{len(models)} node dynamical models   (edge width ∝ studies; arrow = uses node dynamical model)",
         labelloc="b", fontsize="9.5", fontcolor="#6a6a6a")
display(SVG(dot.pipe(format="svg").decode("utf-8")))
```

::: {.notes}
**The map:** where whole-brain *dynamical* modeling has reached patients — ICD-11 chapter → clinical domain → the node model each area uses.

- Counts = distinct, PubMed-verified applications; model leaves read from each study's full text
- **Most areas = neural-mass / mean-field:** Epileptor (epilepsy) · Jansen-Rit + Reduced Wong-Wang / dynamic mean-field (Alzheimer's) · Stefánescu-Jirsa 3D (stroke) · Dody next-gen mass (Parkinson's) · DCM canonical microcircuit (schizophrenia)
- **Two step outside that family:** Hopf / Stuart-Landau oscillator (schizophrenia perturbation) · spiking E/I network (Alzheimer's 1/f marker)
- **Note:** Alzheimer's (12) is now level with epilepsy (12)
:::

## Epilepsy — the Virtual Epileptic Patient {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 8A6Z · MeSH Epilepsy · 12 dynamical-model applications**

::::: {.cards .fill style="--cols:4; --cardw:1fr; --rowh:175px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Jirsa2017.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Jirsa 2017** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

Builds the individualized Virtual Epileptic Patient from patient SEEG to inform therapy [@Jirsa2017]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Proix2017.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Proix 2017** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

Patient-specific virtual brain models from diffusion MRI predict individual seizure propagation and post-surgical variability [@Proix2017]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Proix2018.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Proix 2018** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

A neural-field model of wave propagation + coupled oscillators explains seizure spread/termination, validated on patients [@Proix2018]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/An2019.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**An 2019** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

Patient-specific VEP models identify alternative resections *outside* the epileptogenic zone that spare eloquent cortex [@An2019]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Courtiol2020.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Courtiol 2020** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

Whole-brain modeling reveals dynamical mechanisms of altered interictal resting-state connectivity in patients [@Courtiol2020]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Vattikonda2021.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Vattikonda 2021** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

Bayesian inference on a personalized brain network model identifies spatio-temporal seizure-propagation patterns [@Vattikonda2021]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Makhalova2022.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Makhalova 2022** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

VEP brain modeling relates model parameters to seizure onset zones and surgical outcome [@Makhalova2022]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Dollomaja2023.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Dollomaja 2023** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

Personalized whole-brain models investigate the dynamics and mechanisms of status epilepticus in patients [@Dollomaja2023]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Wang2023.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Wang 2023** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

Personalized brain modeling with imaging data delineates epileptogenic networks in drug-resistant epilepsy [@Wang2023]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Azilinon2024.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Azilinon 2024** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

Brain sodium MRI-derived priors improve personalized model-based estimation of epileptogenic zones in epilepsy patients [@Azilinon2024]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Cai2024.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Cai 2024** · [Wendling NMM]{.mdl}

A functional coupled neural mass model predicts radiofrequency thermocoagulation surgical outcomes in refractory focal epilepsy [@Cai2024]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Dollomaja2025.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Dollomaja 2025** · [Epileptor]{.mdl}

Generates and evaluates a virtual epilepsy patient cohort using personalized whole-brain modeling [@Dollomaja2025]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**The deepest area — 12 applications, almost all Epileptor; the Virtual Epileptic Patient (VEP).**

- **Jirsa 2017** — first personalized VEP in one bitemporal patient (SC + epileptogenic zone + lesions), fit & validated vs SEEG
- **Proix 2017** — dMRI patient-specific models simulate seizure propagation across 15 drug-resistant patients; links to surgical success
- **Proix 2018** — neural-field model: excitable-media waves + coupled oscillators explain seizure spread & termination
- **An 2019** — finds safe surgical targets *outside* the epileptogenic zone, sparing eloquent cortex
- **Courtiol 2020** — interictal: lower *global* excitability, but locally hyperexcitable epileptogenic regions
- **Vattikonda 2021** — Bayesian inference → patient-specific propagation; validated on 25 patients
- **Makhalova 2022** — VEP across 53 patients; params ↔ seizure-onset zones & surgical outcome
- **Wang 2023** — VEP + Bayesian inference on 53 drug-resistant focal patients → epileptogenic-zone networks
- **Dollomaja 2023** — extends to status epilepticus; propagation depends on connectome + network state
- **Azilinon 2024** — adds sodium-²³ MRI priors → better epileptogenic-zone estimation
- **Cai 2024** — *Wendling* NMM (not Epileptor); virtual thermocoagulation on 12 patients predicts outcomes
- **Dollomaja 2025** — openly releases a 30-patient virtual cohort with ground-truth zones, validated vs intracranial

**Arc:** single patient → cohorts → Bayesian inference → new modalities & open virtual cohorts.
:::

## Alzheimer's disease & dementia {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 8A20 · MeSH Alzheimer Disease · 12 dynamical-model applications**

::::: {.cards .fill style="--cols:4; --cardw:1fr; --rowh:175px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Penny2018.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Penny 2018** · [DCM (Jansen-Rit)]{.mdl}

DCM characterizes effective-connectivity changes in preclinical autosomal-dominant AD [@Penny2018]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Zimmermann2018a.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Zimmermann 2018** · [Reduced Wong-Wang]{.mdl}

Personalized Virtual Brain models of 124 individuals across the aging–MCI–AD spectrum predict cognition beyond the empirical connectome [@Zimmermann2018a]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Stefanovski2019.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Stefanovski 2019** · [Jansen-Rit]{.mdl}

TVB links β-amyloid burden to EEG slowing and simulates memantine pharmacodynamics in virtual AD brains [@Stefanovski2019]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Arbabyazd2021.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Arbabyazd 2021** · [Reduced Wong-Wang]{.mdl}

Generates virtual connectomic datasets in AD and aging via whole-brain network dynamics [@Arbabyazd2021]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Monteverdi2022.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Monteverdi 2022** · [Reduced Wong-Wang]{.mdl}

Virtual brain modeling identifies subject-specific excitation/inhibition profiles in neurodegeneration [@Monteverdi2022]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Triebkorn2022.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Triebkorn 2022** · [Jansen-Rit]{.mdl}

The Virtual Brain simulation features augment machine-learning classification of dementia patients [@Triebkorn2022]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/MartinezCanada2023.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Martinez-Cañada 2023** · [Spiking E/I network (LIF)]{.mdl}

Aperiodic 1/f EEG/MEG slopes + brain simulation derive an excitation/inhibition imbalance marker in AD [@MartinezCanada2023]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Monteverdi2023.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Monteverdi 2023** · [Reduced Wong-Wang]{.mdl}

Virtual brain simulations reveal network-specific parameters distinguishing neurodegenerative dementias [@Monteverdi2023]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Patow2023.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Patow 2023** · [dynamic mean-field]{.mdl}

A whole-brain dynamic model shows amyloid-β dominates early (MCI), tau dominates late, via altered E/I balance [@Patow2023]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/CabreraAlvarez2024.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Cabrera-Álvarez 2024** · [Jansen-Rit]{.mdl}

Closed-loop heterodimer model couples Aβ/tau spread to a Jansen-Rit network; inhibition changes (mainly Aβ) drive AD functional-connectivity alterations [@CabreraAlvarez2024]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Amato2024.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Amato 2024** · [Jansen-Rit]{.mdl}

Personalized brain modeling of AD progression estimates neurodegeneration severity from individual EEG [@Amato2024]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Amato2025.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Amato 2025** · [Jansen-Rit]{.mdl}

Brain digital twins + non-invasive recordings enable early diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease [@Amato2025]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**Now level with epilepsy — 12 applications; branches by mechanism & modality.**

- **Penny 2018** · DCM/Jansen-Rit — EEG in presymptomatic PSEN1 carriers; connectivity predicts later MMSE
- **Zimmermann 2018** · Reduced Wong-Wang — TVB across 124 (aging→MCI→AD); params beat the empirical connectome at predicting cognition
- **Stefanovski 2019** · Jansen-Rit — PET amyloid-β modulates E/I → reproduces EEG slowing + memantine reversal
- **Arbabyazd 2021** · RWW — virtual FC ↔ SC; classifiers on virtual connectomes match empirical
- **Monteverdi 2022** · RWW — subject-specific E/I separates neurodegenerative phenotypes
- **Monteverdi 2023** · RWW — AD vs frontotemporal dementia: disease-specific connectivity & synaptic differences
- **Triebkorn 2022** — PET/MRI + TVB-simulated LFPs lift ML classification ~10%
- **Martínez-Cañada 2023** · spiking LIF — 1/f EEG/MEG slopes infer regional E/I abnormalities
- **Patow 2023** · dynamic mean-field — disentangles amyloid (early MCI) vs tau (later AD)
- **Cabrera-Álvarez 2024** · Jansen-Rit — closed-loop heterodimer Aβ/tau spread ↔ network; inhibition (mainly Aβ) drives FC changes; decouples cellular hyperactivity from interregional hypersynchrony
- **Amato 2024** · Jansen-Rit — EEG digital twin separates MCI from healthy, flags CSF hallmarks
- **Amato 2025** · DADD twin — scales to 124: 88% accuracy for CSF-positive, 87% predicting conversion

**Arc:** connectivity & E/I → molecule-to-network (amyloid/tau) → EEG digital twins for early diagnosis.
:::

## Schizophrenia {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 6A20 · MeSH Schizophrenia · 3 dynamical-model applications**

::::: {.cards .center style="--cardw:340px; --cardmax:390px; --rowh:500px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Yang2015.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Yang 2015** · [Reduced Wong-Wang]{.mdl}

A cortical-hierarchy model (E/I gradient) explains preferential connectivity disturbances along the hierarchy in schizophrenia [@Yang2015]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Adams2022.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Adams 2022** · [DCM canonical microcircuit]{.mdl}

DCM of EEG+fMRI shows a consistent loss of pyramidal-cell synaptic gain in schizophrenia [@Adams2022]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Mana2023.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Mana 2023** · [Hopf (Stuart-Landau)]{.mdl}

An *in silico* whole-brain perturbational approach identifies critical brain areas in schizophrenia [@Mana2023]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**Same disease, three models, a moving question.**

- **Yang 2015** · RWW — 161 patients; elevated E/I balance predicts hyperconnectivity in association cortices, tracks symptoms
- **Adams 2022** · DCM canonical microcircuit — EEG+fMRI; greater pyramidal-cell self-inhibition; disinhibition predicts psychotic symptoms
- **Mana 2023** · Hopf/Stuart-Landau — 47 patients vs 118 controls; perturbation reveals temporo-parietal & medial fronto-temporal drivers

**Arc:** circuit E/I → cell-level self-inhibition → spatial drivers of whole-brain dynamics.
:::

## Parkinson's disease {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 8A00.0 · MeSH Parkinson Disease · 4 dynamical-model applications**

::::: {.cards .fill style="--cols:4; --cardw:1fr; --rowh:430px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Maith2020.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Maith 2020** · [basal-ganglia model]{.mdl}

A computational basal-ganglia model fit to Parkinson's patients dissociates the dopamine-dependent pathway changes underlying altered reinforcement learning [@Maith2020]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Jung2022.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Jung 2022** · [Jansen-Rit]{.mdl}

Whole-brain dynamical modeling of individual connectomes classifies Parkinson's patients [@Jung2022]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Meier2022.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Meier 2022** · [mean-field + spiking BG]{.mdl}

Virtual deep brain stimulation: multiscale co-simulation of a spiking basal-ganglia network with a whole-brain mean-field model in The Virtual Brain [@Meier2022]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Angiolelli2025.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Angiolelli 2025** · [Dody next-gen NMM]{.mdl}

A dopamine-dependent whole-brain neural-mass model infers patient-specific dopaminergic tone from EEG/deep-electrode data, distinguishing ON vs OFF L-Dopa [@Angiolelli2025]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**Four very different attacks on the same pathology.**

- **Maith 2020** · spiking basal-ganglia — fit to BOLD between BG nuclei; recovers PD firing-rate & connectivity changes
- **Jung 2022** · Jansen-Rit — whole-brain simulation + behavioural fitting → ML classification of patients vs controls
- **Meier 2022** · mean-field + spiking BG — co-simulated *virtual DBS* shifts thalamus–BG firing toward healthy, alters cortex
- **Angiolelli 2025** · Dody next-gen NMM — EEG + STN electrodes, 10 patients; infers higher ON / lower OFF dopaminergic tone (ON vs OFF L-Dopa)

**Arc:** BG nuclei → whole-brain classification → virtual DBS → dopamine-aware model on intracranial data.
:::

## Stroke {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 8B20 · MeSH Stroke · 2 dynamical-model applications**

::::: {.cards .center style="--cardw:340px; --cardmax:580px; --rowh:500px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Falcon2015.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Falcon 2015** · [Stefánescu-Jirsa 3D]{.mdl}

The Virtual Brain models individual biological correlates of recovery after chronic stroke [@Falcon2015]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Falcon2016a.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Falcon 2016** · [Stefánescu-Jirsa 3D]{.mdl}

TVB modeling of 20 stroke patients links reduced conduction velocity and altered local dynamics/inhibition to motor recovery [@Falcon2016a]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**Same group, same model — from network topology to recovery mechanism.**

- **Falcon 2015** · Stefánescu-Jirsa 3D — 20 patients/10 controls; long-range coupling ↔ global efficiency (not centrality)
- **Falcon 2016** · Stefánescu-Jirsa 3D — 20 patients/11 controls; reduced conduction velocity + reduced local inhibition, tied to **motor recovery**

**Arc:** 2015 = coupling vs efficiency; 2016 = richer params linked to a clinical outcome.
:::

## Brain tumor / glioma {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 2A00.0 · MeSH Glioma · 2 dynamical-model applications**

::::: {.cards .center style="--cardw:340px; --cardmax:580px; --rowh:500px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Aerts2018.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Aerts 2018** · [Reduced Wong-Wang]{.mdl}

Individually-optimized RWW parameters improve FC prediction and differentiate tumor-affected from healthy regions in 25 patients [@Aerts2018]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Aerts2020.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Aerts 2020** · [Reduced Wong-Wang]{.mdl}

TVB *virtual neurosurgery*: model parameters are stable across resection and predict post-operative dynamics [@Aerts2020]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**Same framework, from diagnosis to the surgical trajectory.**

- **Aerts 2018** · RWW — 25 patients/11 controls; individualized params improve FC prediction, flag tumor-affected regions
- **Aerts 2020** · RWW — params stable across resection; *virtual neurosurgery* improves post-op FC in only some patients (a nuanced, patient-specific result)

**Arc:** individualized fitting for differentiation → longitudinal, treatment-oriented use around surgery.
:::

## Multiple sclerosis {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 8A40 · MeSH Multiple Sclerosis · 1 dynamical-model application**

::::: {.cards .center style="--cardw:340px; --cardmax:660px; --rowh:500px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/MartiJuan2023.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Martí-Juan 2023** · [Reduced Wong-Wang]{.mdl}

The Virtual Brain studies structure–function connectivity relationships in multiple-sclerosis patients across multiple centers [@MartiJuan2023]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**Single study — large, multi-site, cognition-focused.**

- **Martí-Juan 2023** · Reduced Wong-Wang — 513 MS patients + 208 controls across 7 centers
- **Finding:** higher structure–function coupling ↔ cognitive impairment (low Symbol Digit Modalities Test)
- **Takeaway:** structure–function coupling from a virtual brain links directly to a clinical cognitive outcome
:::

## Traumatic brain injury {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 NA07 · MeSH Brain Injuries, Traumatic · 1 dynamical-model application**

::::: {.cards .center style="--cardw:340px; --cardmax:660px; --rowh:500px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Good2022.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Good 2022** · [dynamic mean-field]{.mdl}

Personalized connectome-based (TVB) modeling in semi-acute traumatic-brain-injury patients relates model parameters to acute imaging and 6-month outcomes [@Good2022]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**Single study — model recovers hidden parameters with prognostic value.**

- **Good 2022** · dynamic mean-field — 44 TBI patients, resting-state fMRI
- **Finding:** lower regional *inhibitory* connection strength ↔ more symptoms
- **Prognostic:** the same inhibitory params predict worse cognitive outcome at 6 months
:::

## Disorders of consciousness {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 8E2Z · MeSH Consciousness Disorders · 1 dynamical-model application**

::::: {.cards .center style="--cardw:340px; --cardmax:660px; --rowh:500px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Luppi2022a.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Luppi 2022** · [dynamic mean-field]{.mdl}

Whole-brain modeling reveals distinct but convergent mechanistic paths to unconsciousness in anesthesia and disorders of consciousness [@Luppi2022a]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**Single study — one model separates look-alike states by mechanism.**

- **Luppi 2022** · dynamic mean-field — perturbed whole-brain model grounded in PET GABA + diffusion-MRI connectivity
- **Finding:** two distinct routes to unconsciousness — increased local inhibition (anaesthesia) vs randomised connectivity (disorders of consciousness)
- **Takeaway:** the right modalities let one mechanistic model split superficially similar clinical states
:::

## Depression {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 6A70 · MeSH Depressive Disorder, Major · 2 dynamical-model applications**

::::: {.cards .center style="--cardw:340px; --cardmax:580px; --rowh:500px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/An2022.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**An 2022** · [Jansen-Rit]{.mdl}

High-resolution virtual brain modeling personalizes **deep brain stimulation** for treatment-resistant depression via fiber-pathway stimulation responses [@An2022]
:::
::::
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Hofsaehs2026.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Hofsähs 2026** · [Jansen-Rit]{.mdl}

The Virtual Brain links TMS-evoked potentials to inhibitory-neurotransmitter changes in major depressive disorder [@Hofsaehs2026]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**Both Jansen-Rit, different angles on the pathology.**

- **An 2022** · Jansen-Rit — high-res personalized model (>100k stimulable fiber tracts); simulates **DBS** for treatment-resistant depression; ERPs depend on *where* you stimulate
- **Hofsähs 2026** · Jansen-Rit — simulates **TMS**-evoked potentials; GABAergic deficits raise TEP toward MDD-like levels → points to inhibitory targets

**Arc:** from "where to stimulate" (structural model) → "which inhibitory mechanism drives it".
:::

## Obsessive-compulsive disorder {.smaller .cards-slide}

**ICD-11 6B20 · MeSH Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder · 1 dynamical-model application**

::::: {.cards .center style="--cardw:340px; --cardmax:660px; --rowh:500px"}
:::: {.card}
![](img/figures/clinical/Naze2025.jpg){.cardimg}

::: {.cardbody}
**Naze 2025** · [Reduced Wong-Wang]{.mdl}

Computational modeling of OCD patient data identifies frontostriatal-coupling interventions that improve function and predict symptom change [@Naze2025]
:::
::::
:::::

::: {.notes}
**Single study — a directional, circuit-specific intervention.**

- **Naze 2025** · Reduced Wong-Wang — combines neuroimaging + behaviour + modelling
- **Finding:** ↓ ventromedial frontostriatal coupling **and** ↑ dorsolateral cortico-striatal coupling gives the biggest functional improvement
- **Takeaway:** turn one pathway down and another up — a targeted, model-derived treatment hypothesis
:::

<!-- TAKEAWAYS -->
## The bottom line {.smaller}

<style>
.bl-stats { text-align:center; font-size:1.7em; font-weight:700; color:#385a86;
            letter-spacing:0.01em; margin:0.1em 0 0.35em; }
.bl-claim { text-align:center; margin:0 0 0.4em; }
.bl-proven { text-align:center; margin:0 0 0.55em; }
.bl-bets { display:flex; gap:0.7rem; margin:0; align-items:stretch; }
.bl-bets .bet { flex:1 1 0; border:1px solid #d4dde4; border-top:3px solid #6880af;
                border-radius:9px; background:#fff; padding:0.4em 0.6em 0.5em;
                box-shadow:0 1px 5px rgba(40,60,90,.12); font-size:0.8em; line-height:1.28; }
.bl-bets .bet img { display:block; width:100%; height:116px; object-fit:contain;
                    background:#f4f7f9; border-radius:6px; margin:0 0 0.3em; }
.bl-bets .bet figure { margin:0 0 0.3em; }
.bl-bets .bet > :first-child { margin-top:0; }
.bl-bets .bet p { margin:0.18em 0 0; }
.bl-bets .bet strong { color:#23364a; }
.bl-bets .bet .cite, .bl-bets .bet .citation { font-size:0.92em; color:#5a7d80; }
.bl-payoff { text-align:center; font-size:1.3em; margin-top:0.55em; color:#23364a; }
.bl-payoff b { color:#385a86; }
</style>

::: {.bl-stats}
1 recipe · 11 diseases · 41 model applications
:::

::: {.bl-claim}
Brain disorders are disorders of **dynamics** — a personalized virtual brain makes them **measurable** and **treatable**.
:::

:::: {.fragment}
::: {.bl-proven}
The approach has **already reached the clinic** — the **Virtual Epileptic Patient** is in **clinical trials**. So the question is no longer *whether* personalized brain models reach patients, but **where next**.
:::
::::

::::: {.fragment}
:::: {.bl-bets}
::: {.bet}
![](img/figures/clinical/Meier2026_DBS.png)

**Deep brain stimulation**

Target & settings *in silico* before surgery — Parkinson's · depression · OCD [@Meier2026]
:::
::: {.bet}
![](img/figures/clinical/Stefanovski2019.jpg)

**Alzheimer's**

amyloid / tau → network failure → *digital twin* for early diagnosis
:::
::: {.bet}
![](img/figures/clinical/Hofsaehs2026.jpg)

**Mental disorders**

model the *mechanism* — E/I balance, synaptic gain — not a label
:::
::::
:::::

:::: {.fragment}
::: {.bl-payoff}
One model that **explains** the symptom · **predicts** the course · **rehearses** the treatment
:::
::::

::: {.notes}
**Land it on reach and our priorities.**

- **Already clinical:** the Virtual Epileptic Patient is in clinical trials — *a separate, field-leading effort, not our own work*
- **The point is reach:** the same personalized-virtual-brain recipe now spans 11 ICD-11 areas; our focus is the next ones

**Our three bets:**

- **DBS** — clearest translational win: test *where & how* to stimulate before operating (Parkinson's · treatment-resistant depression · OCD)
- **Alzheimer's** — molecule-to-network; now level with epilepsy in real applications; digital twins → early diagnosis
- **Mental disorders** — newest frontier: model the circuit *mechanism*, not the label

**Close:** explain · predict · rehearse.
:::
